title: ShinyHunters, the elusive hacking network that started in France
subhead: Despite multiple arrests, numerous data breaches continue to be claimed under the ShinyHunters banner. Beyond its French roots, the group has now become a cybercriminal 'brand.'

title: At the center of global affairs, Xi keeps Putin waiting on the Power of Siberia 2 pipeline
Israel faces European backlash over Gaza flotilla detentions
Hungary's Magyar signals break with Orban-era diplomacy during Poland visit
subhead: For his first official trip abroad, Hungary's new prime minister chose Poland, making clear his desire to rekindle diplomatic relations with the pro-European government of Donald Tusk. The Hungarian leader is set to visit Vienna on Thursday, then Brussels on Sunday and Monday.

title: Musk eyes Wall Street record with SpaceX IPO
subhead: US media reports say SpaceX is hoping to raise $75 billion and win a valuation of as much as $1.75 trillion when it begins trading as early as next month.

title: Sweden and France become privileged defense partners
subhead: Stockholm announced on Tuesday, May 19, that it wanted to acquire four defense and intervention frigates from the French company Naval Group. This order illustrates the growing strategic ties with Paris, facilitated by the Nordic country's accession to NATO.

title: 'Gaza has become a diplomatic, political and military black hole'
subhead: Donald Trump's promises for the territory, devastated after two years of war, have continued to be proven wrong in the months following the ceasefire with Israel, writes Gilles Paris, columnist for Le Monde, in his column.

'France's president-PM diarchy is shaping the strategy of many 2027 hopefuls'
subhead: The role of prime minister has regained central importance since 2022 and the end of outright majorities in the Assemblée Nationale, observes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

title: Trump sinks lower into nepotism with fund to compensate allies
subhead: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche announced the creation of a fund totaling $1.776 billion for Trump's prosecuted allies and established a 'permanent' ban on tax audits into the president and his family.

title: Cuba's crackdown on dissidents continues, despite US pressure
subhead: In an effort to show good faith to Washington, Havana recently pardoned more than 2,000 detainees. However, most of those who benefited were ordinary delinquents. The Cuban authorities continue to arrest dissidents, bringing the number of political prisoners to 1,260, a record high.
